title: Probability
summary: Chapter 15 introduces theoretical probability based on equally likely outcomes. Students learn to find probability of simple events involving coins, dice, cards, and balls using the formula P(E) = favourable outcomes / total outcomes. The chapter covers complementary events, impossible events (P=0), and certain events (P=1). Problems include playing cards, dice, coloured balls, and real-life situations.
learningPoints: Define probability as a measure of likelihood of an event
State that probability of any event lies between 0 and 1 inclusive
Apply the formula: P(E) = Number of favourable outcomes / Total number of outcomes
Understand that P(impossible event) = 0 and P(certain event) = 1
Identify complementary events and apply P(Ē) = 1 - P(E)
Find probability for single coin, two coins, and three coins
Find probability for single die and two dice
Find probability for playing cards (52 cards, 4 suits, face cards, etc.)
Find probability for selecting balls from a bag
Solve word problems involving probability in real-life contexts

formulas.label: Probability of an Event
formulas.description: Probability = Number of favourable outcomes divided by total number of equally likely outcomes.

formulas.label: Complementary Event
formulas.description: Probability of event NOT occurring equals 1 minus probability of it occurring.

formulas.label: Range of Probability
formulas.description: Probability is always between 0 (impossible) and 1 (certain) inclusive.

formulas.label: Sum of All Probabilities
formulas.description: Probabilities of an event and its complement always add to 1.

resources.label: Probability — Video Lessons
resources.description: Videos on basic probability, coins, dice and cards problems.

mcqs.question: A fair coin is tossed. Probability of getting a head is:
A die is thrown. Probability of getting a number less than 3 is:
A card is drawn from 52 cards. Probability of drawing a king is:
Two coins are tossed. Probability of getting exactly one head is:
mcqs.explanation: Sample space = {HH, HT, TH, TT}. Exactly one head: HT, TH. P = 2/4 = 1/2.

mcqs.question: A bag has 3 red and 5 blue balls. Probability of drawing a red ball:
If P(E) = 0.7, then P(not E) is:
A die is thrown once. Probability of getting an even number is:
From a deck of 52 cards, probability of drawing a red face card is:
mcqs.explanation: Red face cards = 6 (Jack, Queen, King of Hearts and Diamonds). P = 6/52 = 3/26.

mcqs.question: Probability of an impossible event is:
mcqs.explanation: An impossible event has no favourable outcomes. P = 0/n = 0.

mcqs.question: A bag has 5 white, 7 red, 3 black balls. Probability of NOT drawing a white ball:
Two dice are thrown. Probability of getting sum = 7:
A number is chosen from 1 to 20. Probability it is a prime number:
A card is drawn from 52 cards. Probability it is neither a heart nor a king:
mcqs.explanation: Hearts=13, Kings=4, King of hearts counted once. Neither = 52-13-4+1=36. P=36/52=9/13.

mcqs.question: Letters of MATHEMATICS are written on cards. Probability of picking letter M:
mcqs.explanation: MATHEMATICS has 11 letters. M appears 2 times. P = 2/11.

mcqs.question: Three coins are tossed. Probability of getting at least two heads:
mcqs.explanation: Sample space = 8. At least 2 heads: HHH, HHT, HTH, THH = 4. P = 4/8 = 1/2.

exercises.question: A bag has 3 red, 4 blue, 5 white balls. A ball is drawn. Find P(red), P(blue), P(not white).
Two dice are thrown simultaneously. Find probability that sum is 8.
A card is drawn from well-shuffled deck. Find probability: (i) King (ii) Red card (iii) Ace of spades.
Numbers 1 to 90 are written on slips. One is chosen. Find P(multiple of 5), P(perfect square), P(divisible by 7).
A jar contains 24 marbles. Some are green and others are blue. If P(green) = 2/3, find number of blue marbles.
Two players toss a coin alternately. First to get head wins. Player A starts. Find P(A wins).
A game consists of tossing one rupee coin 3 times. Player wins ₹8 if all heads, ₹3 for 2 heads, ₹1 for 1 head, loses ₹1.50 for no heads. Find expected value.
Cards numbered 11 to 60 are put in a box. One card is drawn. Find P(number is a perfect square), P(divisible by 9).
